round1: Round 1 wrapped up, and if you picked Edmonton to win, you're in good company – everyone did, and everyone was wrong. Derrick, Marc, Glenda, Nickall, and Robin showed fleeting moments of insight, but for those who managed zero perfect picks, perhaps 'luck' isn't your strongest stat.
round2: Jamie and Robin get a golf clap for nailing a series, and Marc and Nathan deserve a nod for seeing through the Anaheim mirage that fooled over half the pool. As for Theodore, his repeated attempts at predicting sweeps for the wrong teams were nothing short of a statistical anomaly.
round3: Round 3: Carolina in 5 was a solid pick for some, with Kiersten actually hitting it dead on. But the real story is the absolute clown show that was the Colorado vs Vegas series, where 11 of you proudly picked the wrong team, and none of you saw the sweep coming. Yikes.
round4: Carolina in 6. Five of you got it right, which is good. The other eight who picked Vegas clearly saw a different series, or perhaps just enjoy setting their points on fire.
overall: The 2026 NHL playoffs belong to Derrick, who finally snapped a 20-year title drought with a quiet but ruthlessly efficient 42 points. After winning four times in his first eight years, Derrick spent two decades watching everyone else take turns hoisting the trophy while he collected second-place finishes like participation awards. Nickall made it a two-horse race all the way to the wire at 40 points, but once again proved that being close to the top is his comfort zone. And then there's Benedict -- a three-time runner-up and two-time third-place finisher who somehow managed to finish dead last with 15 points, proving that consistency is only admirable when it's not at the bottom. Carolina's Cup run gave several of you a lifeline, but most of you still managed to fumble it away like a puck in the crease.
